name: Yamanashi Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: noble character, tradition and the violet flowers of the prefecture
purity and the snow-capped summit of Mount Fuji

description: A purple field charged at the centre with a white emblem stylising the kanji 山 and 梨 of Yamanashi as three pointed peaks.
history: The prefectural symbol was adopted in 1966 and used on the prefectural flag with white on a purple field.

features.symbolism: a stylised abstraction of the kanji 山 (yama, 'mountain') and 梨 (nashi, 'pear'), the components of Yamanashi, arranged to evoke a triple-peaked mountain and forward progress.
